selectionPolicy: Reference samples are visual direction cards, not templates. The author may adapt layout rhythm, hierarchy, and interaction affordance, but must not copy the sample verbatim or invent asset ids.

samples.sampleLabel: PC editorial hero with offset copy and one quiet visual anchor
samples.designIntent: Make a brand/story section feel less like an equal card grid by giving one image and one headline clear editorial priority.
samples.layoutSketch: Two-column asymmetric stage: left copy block starts lower than the image, right visual panel is taller, support copy is caption-like below the headline.
samples.htmlSketch:
SERIES

Large calm headline

Short editorial support copy.
samples.interactionCue: Use carousel only if an approved carousel card is present; otherwise keep the section static.
samples.assetCue: Use background-only or object-only image assets only; do not place new text over promo-complete art.

samples.sampleLabel: Mobile editorial hero with image first and caption-like copy
samples.designIntent: Keep the mobile hero premium without forcing a PC split layout into a narrow screen.
samples.layoutSketch: Image card first, then a large two-line headline, a short paragraph, and one low-noise CTA row.
samples.htmlSketch:
NEW MOOD

Mobile editorial title

Readable support copy with enough breathing room.
samples.interactionCue: Avoid hiding hero content behind controls; one swipe cue is enough when carousel is registered.
samples.assetCue: Use mobile-approved or mobile-candidate image variants only as allowed by the packet.

samples.designIntent: Make product discovery feel operational and fast instead of decorative.
samples.layoutSketch: Top row has search/result context, second row has thumb-size filter/sort chips, drawer trigger is visually dominant.
samples.htmlSketch: 128 products
samples.interactionCue: Prefer drawer.filter.basic-v1 when available; visible close affordance is required.
samples.assetCue: Use utility icon family only for filter/sort/action cues.

samples.sampleLabel: Support dashboard with primary task lane
samples.designIntent: Separate high-frequency support tasks from secondary information so the user sees the next action immediately.
samples.layoutSketch: One wide primary card on the left, compact service cards on the right, status/count metadata under each label.
samples.htmlSketch:
Find support fast

Lead action card with product or service lookup.

Repair status
Manuals
samples.interactionCue: Tabs or accordion may be used for grouped services when registered.
samples.assetCue: Use support/care icon families; keep icons consistent inside one section.

samples.designIntent: Keep purchase action reachable while separating reassurance from the primary CTA.
samples.layoutSketch: Sticky bottom bar has price/benefit on the left, one primary CTA on the right, optional small service chip above.
samples.htmlSketch:
Free delivery and installation check

From

Price area
samples.interactionCue: Use sticky.buybox.basic-v1 when available; do not cover required PDP content permanently.
samples.assetCue: Commerce icons can support benefit chips, not replace product media.

samples.sampleLabel: PC PDP summary with action rail and confidence cues
samples.designIntent: Keep product media dominant while making price, benefit, and CTA easy to scan.
samples.layoutSketch: Large product/gallery zone left, right-side summary rail with price stack, benefit chips, and a single primary CTA.
samples.htmlSketch:
Product title

Benefit chips
samples.interactionCue: Use accordion for financing/service details only if registered and useful.
samples.assetCue: Product media stays in gallery; icons are only supporting cues.

samples.designIntent: Use contrast and depth to create a premium first impression without adding extra content.
samples.layoutSketch: Deep background, large central visual, compact copy in a safe corner, small detail chips near the bottom.
samples.htmlSketch:
One decisive product moment

Minimal support copy.
samples.interactionCue: Carousel or compare slider is optional; use only registered interaction cards.
samples.assetCue: Use approved stage/background assets; never stretch small thumbnails into the stage.

samples.sampleLabel: Mobile cinematic hero with safe copy zone
samples.designIntent: Create a premium mobile first view while keeping text readable and controls reachable.
samples.layoutSketch: Tall dark stage, visual area dominates top/middle, copy sits in a bottom safe area with one simple CTA.
samples.htmlSketch:
Premium mobile stage

Short readable support copy.
samples.interactionCue: Keep carousel controls large and visible if a registered carousel is selected.
samples.assetCue: Use mobile viewport image variants only; do not put new text over promo-complete art.

samples.designIntent: Bring premium depth to mobile without making the product area hard to use.
samples.layoutSketch: Dark rounded stage, one large product/media card, short headline, and horizontal detail chips.
samples.htmlSketch:
Immersive product title

Key feature
samples.interactionCue: Carousel swipe cue may be used if carousel is registered; keep controls large.
samples.assetCue: Mobile image variants only; object/product assets should not become noisy backgrounds.

samples.sampleLabel: Mobile soft service hub with one lead path
samples.designIntent: Make service navigation reassuring and guided instead of dense.
samples.layoutSketch: Lead card first with friendly copy, then two-column compact cards, then a checklist-style help path.
samples.htmlSketch:
How can we help?

Lead support path copy.
samples.interactionCue: Accordion or tabs can organize help content when registered.
samples.assetCue: Support/care icon family should be soft and consistent across cards.

samples.sampleLabel: PC soft service hub with guided help lane
samples.designIntent: Make service entry feel calm and guided while still giving multiple paths.
samples.layoutSketch: Soft background, wide lead guidance card, three secondary cards, and a compact next-step checklist.
samples.htmlSketch:
Service guide title

Friendly guidance copy.
samples.interactionCue: Use accordion or tabs only for grouped help content; the lead path should remain visible.
samples.assetCue: Use support/care icon families with the same stroke/fill language across all cards.

samples.sampleLabel: Thumb-zone mobile stack with visible drawer controls
samples.designIntent: Avoid PC-like density by turning actions into clear vertical touch targets and bottom/drawer affordances.
samples.layoutSketch: Single-column cards, large row buttons, sticky or drawer trigger in thumb zone, close path visible.
samples.interactionCue: Use drawer, sticky, tabs, or segmented control only when the card exists; never omit close controls.
samples.assetCue: Use mobile nav/action icon families for row cues only.
